# Graphyn Code (@graphyn/code)

## TL;DR

`@graphyn/code` is the open-source TypeScript CLI and capability junction between local agent harnesses and Graphyn's Rust runtime.

## Authority boundary

- Graphyn Code owns command parsing, capability routing, transport adapters, normalized errors, and receipts.
- The Graphyn desktop Rust ACP runtime owns sessions, approvals, execution, and runtime state.
- The Graphyn Rust base binary owns deterministic local knowledge and base capabilities.
- Graphyn Code is a consult proxy and junction. It is not a second runtime authority.

## Current capabilities

- Read-only cross-harness consults for Claude, Codex, and Gemini.
- Experimental opt-in ACP-over-stdio consult transport.
- Rust base CLI routing through `graphyn base`.
- ACL-gated filesystem inspection through `graphyn fs`.
- Schedule, environment, configuration, and external MCP command surfaces.
- Secret stripping, transform receipts, trace propagation, timing, and normalized failures.

## Truth boundary

- The repository builds from source with Bun.
- End-to-end agent communication does not have a public millisecond latency guarantee.
- Low-latency local routing is an engineering target that still requires reproducible benchmarks.
- Workflow visualization and outcome-driven self-improvement are Graphyn product direction, not fully autonomous behavior shipped by this repository alone.

## Quick start

```bash
bun install
bun run build
bun bin/graphyn.js --help
bun run test
```

## Agent rules

1. Preserve the desktop ACP authority boundary.
2. Keep failures explicit and actionable.
3. Keep consults read-only unless a separate governed execution contract authorizes mutation.
4. Never expose secrets or inherit a caller's unrestricted configuration.
5. Add command or adapter tests and report exact verification receipts.

## Public links

- Product: https://graphyn.ai/
- Source: https://github.com/fuego-wtf/graphyn-code
- Issues: https://github.com/fuego-wtf/graphyn-code/issues
- Package: `@graphyn/code`

## Technical references

- Agent Client Protocol: https://agentclientprotocol.com/
- Model Context Protocol: https://modelcontextprotocol.io/
- Bun: https://bun.sh/
- TypeScript: https://www.typescriptlang.org/
